config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh mudah dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, kekuasaan bisa terlalu gampang menyalahgunakan alasan “demi umum”. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dijaga, bukan yang terus-menerus dipaksa tunduk.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights in a free country like the United States. From my perspective, the whole point of rights is to protect people from having their freedoms swept aside whenever the majority or the government claims it is for the common good. I can accept limited restrictions in cases like public safety or emergencies, but those limits need to be narrow, justified, and accountable. If society always comes first, it becomes too easy to excuse abuse and silence unpopular people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan terhadap warga. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi menurut saya harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar individu, terutama saat negara atau mayoritas bisa salah menafsirkan apa yang benar-benar baik bagi semua orang. Pengalaman kita menunjukkan bahwa ketika hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang paling sering dirugikan justru kelompok kecil, minoritas, dan orang biasa yang tidak punya kuasa. Jadi, bagi saya, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dengan perlindungan hak sebagai pagar utamanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. As an American, I am very wary of any argument that lets the government or the majority override rights just by saying it is for the common good, because that has historically been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse. At the same time, I do think there are narrow cases where public safety or emergencies justify limited restrictions, but those limits have to be temporary, specific, and accountable. In my view, a healthy society is not one where individuals are easily sacrificed, but one where individual rights are the foundation that keeps society honest.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena bagi saya di Indonesia, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dijadikan alasan untuk menekan warga, terutama minoritas dan orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana atau keselamatan publik ketika pembatasan sementara bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan wewenang atas nama kebaikan bersama. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari hak individu yang dilindungi kuat, bukan dari pengorbanan individu yang terus dianggap wajar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that idea is dangerous because it gives the government and the majority too much room to justify censorship, discrimination, or abuse in the name of the common good. I do accept that emergencies and public safety can require limited restrictions, but those restrictions need to be narrow, temporary, and accountable. In my view, a healthy society depends on strong individual rights first, because that is what keeps society from trampling the very people it is supposed to serve.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
